1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: THE HOUR HAS come and split is the moon.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: The Last Day came near, and the moon split apart.

1.author: Arberry
1.text: The Hour has drawn nigh: the moon is split.

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: The Hour hath drawn nigh, and the moon hath been rent in sunder.. And if they behold a sign, they turn away and say: magic continuous.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has been cleft asunder (the people of Makkah requested Prophet Muhammad SAW to show them a miracle, so he showed them the splitting of the moon).

1.author: Itani
1.text: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has split.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: The Hour of Resurrection drew near and the moon split asunder.

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has been cleft asunder.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: The hour drew nigh and the moon was rent in twain.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: The Hour has drawn near and the moon is split.

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: The Hour is drawing near, and the moon is split (in two).

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: The Hour has come near, and the moon has split [in two].

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: The Hour of Doom is drawing near and the moon is rent asunder.

1.author: Shakir
1.text: The hour drew nigh and the moon did rend asunder.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: The Last Hour draws near and the moon is split asunder.

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: The Hour (of Judgment) is nigh, and the moon is cleft asunder.

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: But if they see a sign they turn away, and say: "This is the same old lie continuing,"

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And when they see a sign, they turn away and say, “Just a customary magic!”

2.author: Arberry
2.text: Yet if they see a sign they turn away, and they say 'A continuous sorcery!'

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: And if they behold a sign, they turn away and say: "magic continous"

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And if they see a sign, they turn away, and say: "This is continuous magic."

2.author: Itani
2.text: Yet whenever they see a miracle, they turn away, and say, “Continuous magic.”

2.author: Maududi
2.text: (Regardless of any Signs these people see), they turn away and say: “This is an ongoing sorcery.”

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: And if they see an Ayah, they turn away and say: "This is magic, Mustamir."

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: And if they behold a portent they turn away and say: Prolonged illusion.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: If they see a sign, they turn away, and say, ‘An incessant magic!’

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: Yet if they see a sign (the unbelievers) turn their backs and say: 'This is but a continuation of sorcery'

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And if they see a miracle, they turn away and say, "Passing magic."

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: Whenever they see a miracle, they turn away from it and say, "This is just a powerful magic".

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And if they see a miracle they turn aside and say: Transient magic.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Yet, when they see a sign they [who deny the truth] turn their backs and say, "The same old sorcery!"

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: But if they see a Sign, they turn away, and say, "This is (but) transient magic."

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Wain yaraw ayatan yuAAridoo wayaqooloo sihrun mustamirrun

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And deny, and follow their own vain desires; but every matter is determined at its time.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And they denied and followed their own desires, whereas each matter has been decided!

3.author: Arberry
3.text: They have cried lies, and followed their caprices; but every matter is settled.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And they belied, and they followed their lusts; and every affair cometh to a final goal.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: They belied (the Verses of Allah, this Quran), and followed their own lusts. And every matter will be settled [according to the kind of deeds (for the doer of good deeds, his deeds will take him to Paradise, and similarly evil deeds will take their doers to Hell)].

3.author: Itani
3.text: They lied, and followed their opinions, but everything has its time.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: They also gave the lie to (the splitting asunder of the moon) and only followed their desires. Yet everything is destined to reach an end.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: They denied and followed their own lusts. And every matter will be settled.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: They denied (the Truth) and followed their own lusts. Yet everything will come to a decision

3.author: Qarai
3.text: They denied and followed their own desires, and every matter has a setting [appropriate to it].

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: They have belied, and follow their own fancies. But, every issue will be settled!

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And they denied and followed their inclinations. But for every matter is a [time of] settlement.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: They have rejected it and have followed their own desires, but all matters will be settled (by God).

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And they call (it) a lie, and follow their low desires; and every affair has its appointed term.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: They deny the truth and follow their own whims, every matter has its appointed time,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: They reject (the warning) and follow their (own) lusts but every matter has its appointed time.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Wakaththaboo waittabaAAoo ahwaahum wakullu amrin mustaqirrun

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Messages deterring them from evil had come to them,

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And indeed the news which had a lot of deterrence, came to them.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: And there have come to them such tidings as contain a deterrent --

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And assuredly there hath come Unto them tidings wherein is a deterrent.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And indeed there has come to them news (in this Quran) wherein there is (enough warning) to check (them from evil),

4.author: Itani
4.text: And there came to them news containing a deterrent.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Surely there came to them narratives (of the ancient nations) that should suffice to deter (them from transgression),

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: And indeed there has come to them news wherein there is Muzdajar.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And surely there hath come unto them news whereof the purport should deter,

4.author: Qarai
4.text: There have already come to them reports containing admonishment,

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: Tidings containing a deterrent have come to them

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And there has already come to them of information that in which there is deterrence -

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: They have certainly received the kind of news in which there is a lesson and strong words of wisdom,

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And certainly some narratives have come to them wherein is prevention--

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: there has come to them many a tiding wherein there are warnings,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: There have already come to them Recitals wherein there is (enough) to check (them),

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Walaqad jaahum mina alanbai ma feehi muzdajarun

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Containing consummate wisdom; yet warnings were of no avail.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: The pinnacle of wisdom – so how will the Heralds of warning provide any benefit?

5.author: Arberry
5.text: a Wisdom far-reaching; yet warnings do not avail.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Wisdom consummate. But warnings avail not.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Perfect wisdom (this Quran), but (the preaching of) warners benefit them not,

5.author: Itani
5.text: Profound wisdom—but warnings are of no avail.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: narratives that are full of consummate wisdom. But warnings do not avail them.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Perfect wisdom, but warners benefit them not.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Effective wisdom; but warnings avail not.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: [and representing] far-reaching wisdom; but warnings are of no avail!

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: which are full of wisdom; but the warnings do not help.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Extensive wisdom - but warning does not avail [them].

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: but the warnings have proved to have no effect on them.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Consummate wisdom-- but warnings do not avail;

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: profound in wisdom, but all warnings have been of no avail:

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Mature wisdom;- but (the preaching of) Warners profits them not.

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: So turn away from them. When on the Day the crier calls to the painful business,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: Therefore turn away from them; on the day when the announcer will call towards a severe unknown matter –

6.author: Arberry
6.text: So turn thou away from them. Upon the day when the Caller shall call unto a horrible thing,

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Wherefore withdrew thou from them. The Day whereon the sum- moner Will summon mankind Unto a thing unpleasant.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: So (O Muhammad SAW) withdraw from them. The Day that the caller will call (them) to a terrible thing.

6.author: Itani
6.text: So turn away from them. On the Day when the Caller calls to something terrible.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: So turn away from them, (O Prophet). On the Day when a caller shall call them to a thing most terrible,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: So withdraw from them. The Day that the caller will call (them) to a terrible thing.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: So withdraw from them (O Muhammad) on the day when the Summoner summoneth unto a painful thing.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: So turn away from them! The day when the Caller calls to a dire thing,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Therefore, turn away from them. On the Day when the Caller summons them to a terrible thing,

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: So leave them, [O Muhammad]. The Day the Caller calls to something forbidding,

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: (Muhammad), leave them alone. On the day when they will be called to a terrible punishment,

6.author: Shakir
6.text: So turn (your) back on them (for) the day when the inviter shall invite them to a hard task,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: so ignore them. On the Day when the Crier will call out about a horrible event,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Therefore, (O Prophet,) turn away from them. The Day that the Caller will call (them) to a terrible affair,

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Fatawalla AAanhum yawma yadAAu alddaAAi ila shayin nukurin

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: They will come out of the graves with downcast eyes like an expanding swarm of locusts.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: They will come out from the graves with eyes lowered, as if they were spread locusts.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: abasing their eyes, they shall come forth from the tombs as if they were scattered grasshoppers,

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: With looks downcast they will come forth, from the tombs, as though they were locusts scattered abroad.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: They will come forth, with humbled eyes from (their) graves as if they were locusts spread abroad,

7.author: Itani
7.text: Their eyes humiliated, they will emerge from the graves, as if they were swarming locusts.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: with down-cast eyes they shall go forth from their graves, as though they were scattered locusts.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: They will come forth, with humbled eyes from (their) graves as if they were locusts spread abroad,

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: With downcast eyes, they come forth from the graves as they were locusts spread abroad,

7.author: Qarai
7.text: they will emerge from the graves as if they were scattered locusts with a humbled look [in their eyes],

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: their eyes will be humbled as they come out from their graves as if they were scattered locusts,

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Their eyes humbled, they will emerge from the graves as if they were locusts spreading,

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: they will come out of their graves, their eyes cast down, hastening towards their Summoner as locusts rushing about.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Their eyes cast down, going forth from their graves as if they were scattered locusts,

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: with downcast eyes they shall come out of their graves, as if they were locusts milling about

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: They will come forth,- their eyes humbled - from (their) graves, (torpid) like locusts scattered abroad,

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: KhushshaAAan absaruhum yakhrujoona mina alajdathi kaannahum jaradun muntashirun

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: They will hasten forward to the caller, gazes fixed. And the unbelievers will say: "This is the day of untold woe."

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Rushing towards the caller; the disbelievers will say, “This is a tough day.”

8.author: Arberry
8.text: running with outstretched necks to the Caller. The unbelievers shall say, 'This is a hard day!'

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Hastening toward the summoner. The infidels will say: this is a day diffrcult.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Hastening towards the caller, the disbelievers will say: "This is a hard Day."

8.author: Itani
8.text: Scrambling towards the Caller, the disbelievers will say, “This is a difficult Day.”

8.author: Maududi
8.text: They shall be hurrying forth towards the caller, and the unbelievers (who had once denied this Day), will say: “This is a woeful Day.”

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Hastening towards the caller. The disbelievers will say: "This is a hard Day."

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Hastening toward the summoner; the disbelievers say: This is a hard day.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: scrambling toward the summoner. The faithless will say, ‘This is a hard day!’

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: running (with their necks extended) to the Caller. The unbelievers will say: 'This is indeed a harsh Day'

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Racing ahead toward the Caller. The disbelievers will say, "This is a difficult Day."

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: These disbelievers will say, "This is a hard day".

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Hastening to the inviter. The unbelievers shall say: This is a hard day.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: hastening towards the Crier. Those who deny the truth will cry, "This is such a hard day!"

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Hastening, with eyes transfixed, towards the Caller!- "Hard is this Day!", the Unbelievers will say.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: MuhtiAAeena ila alddaAAi yaqoolu alkafiroona hatha yawmun AAasirun

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: The people of Noah had denied before them, and had called Our votary a liar, and said: "He is possessed," and repulsed him.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: Before these, the people of Nooh denied and they belied Our bondman and said, “He is a madman” and rebuffed him.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: The people of Noah cried lies before them; they cried lies to Our servant, and said, 'A man possessed!' And he was rejected.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: There belied before them the people of Nuh. So they belied Our bondman Nuh and said: `a madman;' and moreover he was reproven.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: The people of Nuh (Noah) denied (their Messenger) before them, they rejected Our slave, and said: "A madman!" and he was insolently rebuked and threatened.

9.author: Itani
9.text: Before them the people of Noah disbelieved. They rejected Our servant, and said, “Crazy,” and he was rebuked.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Before them Noah's people also gave the lie (to his Message). They rejected Our servant as a liar, saying: “He is a madman”; and he was rebuffed.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: The people of Nuh denied (their Messenger) before them. They rejected Our servant and said: "A madman!" Wazdujir.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: The folk of Noah denied before them, yea, they denied Our slave and said: A madman; and he was repulsed.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: The people of Noah impugned before them. So they impugned Our servant and said, ‘A crazy man,’ and he was reviled.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: (Long) before them the nation of Noah belied. They belied Our worshiper saying: 'Mad' and he was reprimanded.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and they denied Our servant and said, "A madman," and he was repelled.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: The people of Noah, who lived before them, had also rejected (Our guidance). They rejected Our servant and said, "He is an insane person so let us drive him away".

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Before them the people of Nuh rejected, so they rejected Our servant and called (him) mad, and he was driven away.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: The people of Noah denied [the truth] before them. They belied Our messenger saying, "He is mad!" He was rebuffed,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Before them the People of Noah rejected (their messenger): they rejected Our servant, and said, "Here is one possessed!", and he was driven out.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin fakaththaboo AAabdana waqaloo majnoonun waizdujira

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: So he prayed to his Lord: "I am helpless, deliver me."

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: He therefore prayed to his Lord, “I am overpowered, therefore avenge me.”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: And so he called unto his Lord, saying, 'I am vanquished; do Thou succour me!'

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Thereupon he prayed Unto his Lord: verily am overcome, so vindicate me.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Then he invoked his Lord (saying): "I have been overcome, so help (me)!"

10.author: Itani
10.text: So he appealed to his Lord, “I am overwhelmed, so help me.”

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Then he called upon His Lord: “Verily I am vanquished; so come You to my aid.”

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Then he invoked his Lord (saying): "I have been overcome, so help (me)!"

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: So he cried unto his Lord, saying: I am vanquished, so give help.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Thereat he invoked his Lord, [saying,] ‘I have been overcome, so help [me].’

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Then he supplicated to his Lord, (saying): 'I am overcome, help me'

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: So he invoked his Lord, "Indeed, I am overpowered, so help."

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Noah prayed, "Lord, help me; I am defeated".

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Therefore he called upon his Lord: I am overcome, come Thou then to help.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: so he cried out to his Lord, saying, "I am overcome, so help me!"

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Then he called on his Lord: "I am one overcome: do Thou then help (me)!"

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: And We opened up the flood gates of the sky with water pouring down in torrents,

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: We therefore opened the gates of heaven, with water flowing furiously.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Then We opened the gates of heaven unto water torrential,

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Then We opened the portals of heaven with water poured out.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: So We opened the gates of heaven with water pouring forth.

11.author: Itani
11.text: So We opened the floodgates of heaven with water pouring down.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Thereupon We opened the gates of the sky for water to pour down,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: So, We opened the gates of the heaven with water Munhamir.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Then opened We the gates of heaven with pouring water

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Then We opened the gates of the sky with pouring waters,

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: We opened the Gates of Heaven with torrential water

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Then We opened the gates of the heaven with rain pouring down

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: We opened the gates of the sky and water started to pour down.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: So We opened the gates of the cloud with water pouring

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: So We opened the gates of the sky with water pouring down in torrents,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: So We opened the gates of heaven, with water pouring forth.

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: And We opened up the springs of the earth; and the waters met for a decreed end.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And caused springs to gush out from the earth, so that the two waters met totalling a quantity that had been destined.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: and made the earth to gush with fountains, and the waters met for a matter decreed.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And We made the earth break forth with springs; SO that the water met for an affair already decreed.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And We caused the earth to gush forth with springs. So the waters (of the heaven and the earth) met for a matter predestined.

12.author: Itani
12.text: And We made the earth burst with springs, and the waters met for a purpose already destined.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: and We made the earth burst forth with springs, and all this water converged to fulfil that which had been decreed.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And We caused springs to gush forth from the earth. So, the waters met for a matter predestined.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And caused the earth to gush forth springs, so that the waters met for a predestined purpose.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: and We made the earth burst forth with springs, and the waters met for a preordained purpose.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: and caused the earth to gush with springs, so that the waters met for a predestined matter.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And caused the earth to burst with springs, and the waters met for a matter already predestined.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: We caused the earth to burst forth with springs so that the waters could come together for a predestined purpose.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And We made water to flow forth in the land in springs, so the water gathered together according to a measure already ordained.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: and We caused the earth to burst with gushing springs: so that the waters met for a purpose which had been decreed.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: And We caused the earth to gush forth with springs, so the waters met (and rose) to the extent decreed.

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Wafajjarna alarda AAuyoonan failtaqa almao AAala amrin qad qudira

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: But We bore him on a (vessel made) of planks and oakum,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And We carried Nooh upon a ship of wooden planks and nails.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: And We bore him upon a well-planked vessel well-caulked

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And We bare him on a thing of planks and nails.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And We carried him on a (ship) made of planks and nails,

13.author: Itani
13.text: And We carried him on a craft of planks and nails.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: And We bore Noah on the vessel built of planks and nails,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And We carried him on a (ship) made of planks and nails (Dusur)

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And We carried him upon a thing of planks and nails,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: We bore him on a vessel made of planks and nails,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: We carried him in a well built, watertight vessel made from planks (of wood)

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And We carried him on a [construction of] planks and nails,

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: We carried him, (Noah), on a vessel built with boards fixed together with nails,

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And We bore him on that which was made of planks and nails

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: We bore him on an [ark] which, made of planks and nails,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: But We bore him on an (Ark) made of broad planks and caulked with palm-fibre:

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Which sailed right under Our eyes: A recompense for him who had been denied.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Sailing in front of Our sight; as a reward for the sake of one who was rejected.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: running before Our eyes-a recompense for him denied.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Moving forward under Our eyes: a requital for him who had been rejected.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Floating under Our Eyes, a reward for him who had been rejected!

14.author: Itani
14.text: Sailing before Our eyes; a reward for him who was rejected.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: which sailed on under Our supervision: a reward for him who had been shown ingratitude.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Floating under Our Eyes, a reward for him who had been rejected!

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: That ran (upon the waters) in Our sight, as a reward for him who was rejected.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: which sailed [over the flood waters] in Our sight, as a retribution for him who was met with disbelief.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: that ran on under Our Eyes, a recompense for him because he had been disbelieved.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: Sailing under Our observation as reward for he who had been denied.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: which floated on the water before Our very eyes. The flood was a recompense for the deeds of the disbelievers.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Sailing, before Our eyes, a reward for him who was denied.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: floated on under Our eyes: a vindication of him who had been rejected.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: She floats under our eyes (and care): a recompense to one who had been rejected (with scorn)!

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Tajree biaAAyunina jazaan liman kana kufira

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: And We left it as a sign. Is there anyone who will be warned?

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: And We left it as a sign – so is there one who would ponder?

15.author: Arberry
15.text: And We left it for a sign. Is there any that will remember?

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And assuredly We left it for a sign. Is there then anyone who would be admonished?

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And indeed, We have left this as a sign, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

15.author: Itani
15.text: And We left it as a sign. Is there anyone who would take heed?

15.author: Maududi
15.text: And We left the Ark as a Sign. Is there, then, any who will take heed?

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And indeed, We have left this as a sign. Then is there any that will remember

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: And verily We left it as a token; but is there any that remembereth?

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Certainly We have left it as a sign; so is there anyone who will be admonished?

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: We have left it as a sign. Is there any that will remember?

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And We left it as a sign, so is there any who will remember?

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: We made (the story of Noah) as evidence of the Truth. However, is there anyone who would take heed?

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And certainly We left it as a sign, but is there anyone who

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: We have left this as a sign: but will anyone take heed?

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: And We have left this as a Sign (for all time): then is there any that will receive admonition?

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Walaqad taraknaha ayatan fahal min muddakirin

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: How was then My punishment and My commination!

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: So how did My punishment turn out, and My threats?

16.author: Arberry
16.text: How then were My chastisement and My warnings?

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: So how dreadful have been My torment and My warning!

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Then how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings?

16.author: Itani
16.text: So how were My punishment and My warnings?

16.author: Maududi
16.text: So how awesome were My chastisement and My warnings!

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Then how (terrible) was My torment and My warnings

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Then see how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings!

16.author: Qarai
16.text: So how were My punishment and warnings?

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: How then were My punishment and My warnings!

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: How terrible was My torment and the result of (their disregard) of My warning.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: How (great) was then My punishment and My warning!

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: How terrible then was My punishment and My warning.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: But how (terrible) was My Penalty and My Warning?

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Easy have We made the Qur'an to understand: So is there any one who will be warned?

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And We have indeed made the Qur’an easy to memorise, so is there one who would remember?

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember?

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And assuredly We have made the Qur'an easy for admonition; is there then any one who would be admonished?

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And We have indeed made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

17.author: Itani
17.text: We made the Quran easy to learn. Is there anyone who would learn?

17.author: Maududi
17.text: We have made this Qur'an easy as a reminder. Is there, then, any who will take heed?

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there anyone who will remember

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth?

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished?

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: We have made the Koran easy to remember, is there any that will remember!

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: We have made the Quran easy to understand, but is there anyone who would pay attention?

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind?

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: We have made it easy to learn lessons from the Quran. Is there anyone who would receive admonition?

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition?

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: The 'Ad had also disavowed. How was then My punishment and My commination!

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: The tribe of A’ad denied – so how did My punishment turn out, and My warnings?

18.author: Arberry
18.text: Ad cried lies. How then were My chastisement and My warnings?

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: And there belied the 'A-ad; so how dreadful have been My torment and warning!

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: 'Ad (people) belied (their Prophet, Hud), then how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings?

18.author: Itani
18.text: Aad denied the truth. So how were My punishment and My warnings?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: 'Ad also gave the lie (to Hud). So how awesome were My chastisement and My warnings!

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: `Ad denied; then how was My torment and My warnings

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: (The tribe of) A'ad rejected warnings. Then how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: [The people of] ‘Ad impugned [their apostle]. So how were My punishment and warnings?

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Aad too belied. How then were My punishment and My warnings!

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: 'Aad denied; and how [severe] were My punishment and warning.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: The people of `Ad rejected Our guidance. How terrible was My torment and the result (of their disregard) of My warning.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Ad treated (the truth) as a lie, so how (great) was My punishment and My warning!

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: The people of 'Ad too rejected the truth. How terrible was My punishment and My warning.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: The 'Ad (people) (too) rejected (Truth): then how terrible was My Penalty and My Warning?

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Kaththabat AAadun fakayfa kana AAathabee wanuthuri

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: We let loose against them a violent roaring wind on a day of ill omen, continuous,

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: We indeed sent towards them a severe windstorm, on a day the ill luck of which lasted upon them forever.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: We loosed against them a wind clamorous in a day of ill fortune continuous,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Verily We! We sent against them a raging Wind On a day of calamity continuous.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Verily, We sent against them a furious wind of harsh voice on a day of evil omen and continuous calamity.

19.author: Itani
19.text: We unleashed upon them a screaming wind, on a day of unrelenting misery.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: We sent a tumultuous wind against them on a day of unremitting misfortune,

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Verily, We sent against them a violently cold (Sarsar) wind on a day of calamity, continuous.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Lo! We let loose on them a raging wind on a day of constant calamity,

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Indeed We unleashed upon them an icy gale on an incessantly ill-fated day,

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: And We sent against them a howling wind in a Day of continuous of ill fortune

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: Indeed, We sent upon them a screaming wind on a day of continuous misfortune,

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: On an unfortunate day We sent upon them a continuous violent wind

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Surely We sent on them a tornado in a day of bitter ill-luck

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: We sent a raging wind against them on a day of continuous calamity.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: For We sent against them a furious wind, on a Day of violent Disaster,

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Inna arsalna AAalayhim reehan sarsaran fee yawmi nahsin mustamirrin

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Which snatched away men as though they were palm trees pulled out by the roots.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Smashing down men as if they were uprooted trunks of date palms.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: plucking up men as if they were stumps of uprooted palm-trees.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Carrying men away as though they were trunks of palm-trees uprooted.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Plucking out men as if they were uprooted stems of date-palms.

20.author: Itani
20.text: Plucking the people away, as though they were trunks of uprooted palm-trees.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: which tore people away and hurled them as though they were trunks of uprooted palm-trees.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Plucking out men as if they were uprooted stems of date palms.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Sweeping men away as though they were uprooted trunks of palm-trees.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: knocking down people as if they were trunks of uprooted palm trees.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: and snatched people up as though they were stumps of uprooted palmtrees.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Extracting the people as if they were trunks of palm trees uprooted.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: which hurled people around like uprooted trunks of palm-trees.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Tearing men away as if they were the trunks of palm-trees torn up.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: It swept people away as if they were trunks of uprooted palm trees.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Plucking out men as if they were roots of palm-trees torn up (from the ground).

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: TanziAAu alnnasa kaannahum aAAjazu nakhlin munqaAAirin

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: How was then My punishment and My commination!

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: So how did My punishment turn out, and My warnings?

21.author: Arberry
21.text: How then were My chastisement and My warnings?

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: So how dreodful have been My torment and My warning.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Then, how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings?

21.author: Itani
21.text: So how were My punishment and My warnings?

21.author: Maududi
21.text: So how awesome were My chastisement and My warnings!

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Then, how was My torment and My warnings

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Then see how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings!

21.author: Qarai
21.text: So how were My punishment and warnings?!

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: How then were My punishment and My warnings!

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: How terrible was Our torment and the result of (their disregard) of Our warning.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: How (great) was then My punishment and My warning!

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: How [dreadful] was My punishment and My warning!

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Yea, how (terrible) was My Penalty and My Warning!

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Easy have We made the Qur'an to understand: So is there any one who will pay heed?

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: And We have indeed made the Qur’an easy to memorise, so is there one who would remember?

22.author: Arberry
22.text: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember?

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: And assuredly We have made the Qur'an easy for admonition; is therethen any one who would be admonished!

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: And We have indeed made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

22.author: Itani
22.text: We made the Quran easy to remember. Is there anyone who would remember?

22.author: Maududi
22.text: We have made the Qur'an easy to derive lessons from. Is there, then, any who will take heed?

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth?

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished?

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: We have made the Koran easy to remember, is there any that will remember?

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: We have made the Quran easy to understand, but is there anyone who would pay attention?

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind?

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: We have made it easy to learn lessons from the Quran: is there anyone who would receive admonition?

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: But We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition?

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: The tribe of Thamud denied the Noble Messengers.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: Thamood cried lies to the warnings

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: And the Thamud belied the warnings.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Thamud gave the lie to the warnings,

23.author: Qarai
23.text: [The people of] Thamud denied the warnings,

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: The people of Thamud rejected Our warnings.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: The tribe of Thamud also rejected Our warnings:

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: And said: "Should we follow only one man among us? In that case we shall he in error and insane.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: So they said, “What! Shall we follow a man from among us? If we do, we are indeed astray, and insane!”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: and said, 'What, shall we follow a mortal, one out of ourselves? Then indeed we should be in error and insanity!

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: And they said: a mere humanbeing from amongst us, and single! shall we follow him! verily then we should fall in error and madness.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: For they said: "A man! Alone from among us, that we are to follow? Truly, then we should be in error and distress or madness!"

24.author: Itani
24.text: They said, “Are we to follow one of us, a human being? We would then go astray, and end up in Hell.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: saying: “Are we to follow a single mortal, one from among ourselves? If we do that, we shall surely be in error and folly.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: And they said: "A man, alone among us -- shall we follow him Truly, then we should be in error and distress!"

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: For they said; Is it a mortal man, alone among us, that we are to follow? Then indeed we should fall into error and madness.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: and they said, ‘Are we to follow a lone human from ourselves?! Indeed then we would be in error and madness.’

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: They said: 'Are we to follow a mortal who is one of us? Then indeed, we would surely be in error and insane.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And said, "Is it one human being among us that we should follow? Indeed, we would then be in error and madness.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: They said, "Should we follow only one person among us. We shall be clearly in error and in trouble (if we do so).

24.author: Shakir
24.text: So they said: What! a single mortal from among us! Shall we follow him? Most surely we shall in that case be in sure error and distress:

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: they said, "Are we to follow a man from amongst ourselves? We would surely then fall into error and madness.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: For they said: "What! a man! a Solitary one from among ourselves! shall we follow such a one? Truly should we then be straying in mind, and mad!

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Faqaloo abasharan minna wahidan nattabiAAuhu inna ithan lafee dalalin wasuAAurin

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Was he the one of all of us to have been given the exposition? He is surely an impudent liar."

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: “What! Of all the men among us, the remembrance has come down upon him? In fact, he is a mischievous, great liar.”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Has the Reminder been cast upon him alone among us? Nay, rather he is an impudent liar.'

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Hath the Admonition been laid upon him from amongst us! Aye! he is a liar insolent.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: "Is it that the Reminder is sent to him [Prophet Salih (Saleh)] alone from among us? Nay, he is an insolent liar!"

25.author: Itani
25.text: Was the message given to him, out of all of us? In fact, he is a wicked liar.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Was there none but he to whom the Reminder could be vouchsafed excluding all others? Nay; he is an insolent liar.”

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: "Is it that the Reminder is sent to him alone from among us Nay, he is an insolent liar!"

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Hath the remembrance been given unto him alone among us? Nay, but he is a rash liar.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: ‘Has the Reminder been cast upon him from among us? No, he is a self-conceited liar.’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: Out of all of us has the Reminder been given to him alone? Rather, he is indeed an arrogant liar'

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: Has the message been sent down upon him from among us? Rather, he is an insolent liar."

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: How is it that he has received guidance? In fact, he is the most untruthfull and arrogant person".

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Has the reminder been made to light upon him from among us? Nay! he is an insolent liar!

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Has the [Divine] message been revealed to him alone of all of us? No, he is a boastful liar."

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: "Is it that the Message is sent to him, of all people amongst us? Nay, he is a liar, an insolent one!"

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Aolqiya alththikru AAalayhi min baynina bal huwa kaththabun ashirun

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: "Tomorrow they will know who is the impudent liar!

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: It was said to Saleh, “They will soon realise tomorrow who is the mischievous great liar.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: 'They shall surely know tomorrow who is the impudent liar.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: They shall know tomorrow whichsoever is a liar insolent.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Tomorrow they will come to know, who is the liar, the insolent one!

26.author: Itani
26.text: They will know tomorrow who the wicked liar is.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: (We told Our Messenger): “Tomorrow they shall know who is the insolent liar.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Tomorrow they will come to know who is the liar, the insolent one!

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: (Unto their warner it was said): To-morrow they will know who is the rash liar.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: ‘Tomorrow they will know who is a self-conceited liar.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: (To him We said): 'Tomorrow they shall know who is the arrogant liar.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: They will know tomorrow who is the insolent liar.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: (We told Salih), "Tomorrow they will know who is the most arrogant liar.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Tomorrow shall they know who is the liar, the insolent one.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: [We said to him] "Tomorrow they shall know who is the boastful liar,

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: Ah! they will know on the morrow, which is the liar, the insolent one!

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: We shall send the she-camel to try them. So watch them, and be constant.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: “We shall send a she-camel to test them, therefore O Saleh, wait and have patience.”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: They shall send the She-camel as a trial for them; so watch thou them and keep patience.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Verify We are sending the she-camel as a test for them, so watch them thou and have patience.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them [O Salih (Saleh)], and be patient!

27.author: Itani
27.text: We are sending the she-camel as a test for them; so watch them and be patient.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: We shall send the she-camel as a trial for them; so watch their end with patience.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them. So watch them, and be patient!

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Lo! We are sending the she-camel as a test for them; so watch them and have patience;

27.author: Qarai
27.text: We are sending the She-camel as a trial for them; so watch them and be steadfast.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: We are sending to them as a trial, a shecamel, so watch them and have patience.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Indeed, We are sending the she-camel as trial for them, so watch them and be patient.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: We are sending the she-camel to them to test them. So watch them and be patient.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Surely We are going to send the she-camel as a trial for them; therefore watch them and have patience.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: for We are [going to] send the she-camel as a trial for them, so watch them and be patient.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: For We will send the she-camel by way of trial for them. So watch them, (O Salih), and possess thyself in patience!

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Inna mursiloo alnnaqati fitnatan lahum fairtaqibhum waistabir

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Tell them that the water is to be apportioned between them (and her); and every turn of drinking will be fixed (for each)."

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: “And inform them that the water is to be shared between them; only those may come whose turn it is.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: And tell them that the water is to be divided between them, each drink for each in turn.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And declare thou Unto them that water hath been divided between them; every drinking shall be by turns.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them. Each one's right to drink being established (by turns).

28.author: Itani
28.text: And inform them that the water is to be shared between them; each share of drink made available.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Let them know that the water should be divided between them and the she-camel, each availing their turn.”

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them, each one's right to drink being established (by turns).

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: And inform them that the water is to be shared between (her and) them. Every drinking will be witnessed.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: Inform them that the water is to be shared between them; each of them showing up at his turn.’

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: Tell them that the water is to be divided between them, a drink each for them in turn'

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And inform them that the water is shared between them, each [day of] drink attended [by turn].

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Tell them that each one of them has the right to have a certain share of water".

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And inform them that the water is shared between them; every share of the water shall be regulated.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: And tell them that the water [of the well] is to be divided between them, and that each one should drink in turn."

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: And tell them that the water is to be divided between them: Each one's right to drink being brought forward (by suitable turns).

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Wanabbihum anna almaa qismatun baynahum kullu shirbin muhtadarun

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: But they called their commander, who seized and hamstrung her.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: In response they called their companion – he therefore caught and hamstrung the she-camel.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Then they called their comrade, and he took in hand, and hamstrung her.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Then they called their fellow, and he took the sword and hamstrung her,

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: But they called their comrade and he took (a sword) and killed (her).

29.author: Itani
29.text: But they called their friend, and he dared, and he slaughtered.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: Eventually, they summoned their companion, and he undertook the (outrageous) task and hamstrung the she-camel.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: But they called their comrade and he took (a sword) and killed (her).

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: But they call their comrade and he took and hamstrung (her).

29.author: Qarai
29.text: But they called their companion, and he took [a knife] and hamstrung [her].

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: But they called their companion who took hold of her and hamstrung her.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: But they called their companion, and he dared and hamstrung [her].

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: They called together their companions and agreed to slay the she-camel.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: But they called their companion, so he took (the sword) and slew (her).

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: But they called their companion who took a sword and hamstrung her.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: But they called to their companion, and he took a sword in hand, and hamstrung (her).

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: How was then My punishment of which they had been warned?

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: So how did My punishment turn out, and My warnings?

30.author: Arberry
30.text: How then were My chastisement and My warnings?

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: So how dreadful have been My torment and My warnings.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Then, how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings?

30.author: Itani
30.text: So how were My punishment and My warnings?

30.author: Maududi
30.text: So how awesome were My chastisement and My warnings!

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: Then, how was My torment and My warnings

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Then see how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings!

30.author: Qarai
30.text: So how were My punishment and My warnings?!

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: How then were My punishment and My warnings!

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: How terrible was My punishment and warning.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: How (great) was then My punishment and My warning!

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: How [terrible] were My punishment and My warnings!

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Ah! how (terrible) was My Penalty and My Warning!

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: We sent a single blast against them, and they were reduced to husks of a decayed fence.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: Indeed We sent upon them a single Scream – thereupon they became like the barrier builder’s residual dry trampled hay.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: We loosed against them one Cry, and they were as the wattles of a pen-builder.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Verily We We sent upon them one shout, and they became as the stubble of a fold-builder.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: Verily, We sent against them a single Saihah (torment - awful cry, etc.), and they became like the dry stubble of a fold-builder.

31.author: Itani
31.text: We sent against them a single Scream, and they became like crushed hay.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: Behold, We sent a single Blast against them, and they became like the trampled twigs of a sheep pen-builder.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: Verily, We sent against them a single Sayhah, and they became like straw Al-Muhtazir.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Lo! We sent upon them one Shout, and they became as the dry twigs (rejected by) the builder of a cattle-fold.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: We sent against them a single Cry, and they became like the dry sticks of a corral builder.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: Then We sent against them one Shout and they became like the trampled twigs of the (sheep) penbuilder.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: Indeed, We sent upon them one blast from the sky, and they became like the dry twig fragments of an [animal] pen.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: We sent upon them a single blast of sound and they were left like hay to be used by the cattle.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Surely We sent upon them a single cry, so they were like the dry fragments of trees which the maker of an enclosure collects.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: Then We sent a single blast against them and they became like dry stubble which has been trampled upon.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: For We sent against them a single Mighty Blast, and they became like the dry stubble used by one who pens cattle.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Inna arsalna AAalayhim sayhatan wahidatan fakanoo kahasheemi almuhtathiri

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: Easy have We made the Qur'an to understand: So is there any one who will be warned?

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And We have indeed made the Qur’an easy to memorise, so is there one who would remember?

32.author: Arberry
32.text: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember?

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And assuredly We have made the Qur'an eary for admanirion: is there then any one who would be admonished.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And indeed, We have made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

32.author: Itani
32.text: We made the Quran easy to understand. Is there anyone who would understand?

32.author: Maududi
32.text: Surely We have made this Qur'an easy as a reminder. Is there, then, any who will take heed?

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And indeed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth?

32.author: Qarai
32.text: Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished?

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: We have made the Koran an easy Remembrance, is there any that will remember!

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: We made the Quran easy to understand, but is there anyone who would take heed?

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind?

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Indeed, We have made the Quran easy to learn lessons from. Is there anyone who would receive admonition?

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition?

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: The people of Lot rejected the warnings.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: The people of Lut denied the Noble Messengers.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: The people of Lot cried lies to the warnings.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: The people of Lut belied the warnings.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: The people of Lout (Lot) belied the warnings.

33.author: Itani
33.text: The people of Lot rejected the warnings.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: Lot's people also gave the lie to the warnings,

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: The people of Lut denied the warnings.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: The folk of Lot rejected warnings.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: And the people of Lot denied the warnings.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: The nation of Lot belied Our warnings.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: The people of Lot denied the warning.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: The people of Lot rejected Our warning.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: The people of Lut treated the warning. as a lie.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: The people of Lot rejected Our warnings.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: The people of Lut rejected (his) warning.

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: We sent a stone-hurtling storm against them, except the family of Lot whom We saved in the early hours of the morning

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: Indeed We sent a shower of stones upon them, except the family of Lut; We rescued them before dawn.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: We loosed against them a squall of pebbles except the folk of Lot; We delivered them at the dawn --

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Verily We! We sent upon them a gravel-storm save the household of Lut; them We delivered at early dawn.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: Verily, We sent against them a violent storm of stones (which destroyed them all), except the family of Lout (Lot), whom We saved in last hour of the night,

34.author: Itani
34.text: We unleashed upon them a shower of stones, except for the family of Lot; We rescued them at dawn.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: and behold, We let loose upon them a tempest which rained stones upon them, except upon Lot's household whom We rescued in the last hours of the night

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: Verily, We sent against them Hasib (a violent storm of stones), except the family of Lut, them We saved in the last hour of the night,

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Lo! We sent a storm of stones upon them (all) save the family of Lot, whom We rescued in the last watch of the night,

34.author: Qarai
34.text: We unleashed upon them a rain of stones, excepting the family of Lot, whom We delivered at dawn

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: We let loose on all of them a squall of claystones, except for the house of Lot whom We saved at dawn

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: Indeed, We sent upon them a storm of stones, except the family of Lot - We saved them before dawn

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: We sent down upon them a violent sandstorm (which destroyed them all) except for the family of Lot, whom We saved in the early morning by Our favor.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Surely We sent upon them a stonestorm, except Lut's followers; We saved them a little before daybreak,

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: We sent a sandstorm against them which destroyed them all, except the family of Lot, whom We saved at the break of dawn,

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: We sent against them a violent Tornado with showers of stones, (which destroyed them), except Lut's household: them We delivered by early Dawn,-

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Inna arsalna AAalayhim hasiban illa ala lootin najjaynahum bisaharin

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: As a favour from Us. That is how We reward the grateful.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: As a reward from Us; this is how We reward one who gives thanks.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: a blessing from Us; even so We recompense him who is thankful.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: As a favour from us. Thus We recompense him who giveth thanks.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: As a Favour from Us, thus do We reward him who gives thanks (by obeying Us).

35.author: Itani
35.text: A blessing from Us. Thus We reward the thankful.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: as a favour from Us. Thus do We reward those who give thanks.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: As a favor from Us. Thus do We reward him who gives thanks.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: As grace from Us. Thus We reward him who giveth thanks.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: as a blessing from Us. Thus do We reward those who give thanks.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: through Our Mercy. So it is that We recompense the thankful.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: As favor from us. Thus do We reward he who is grateful.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: Thus do We recompense the grateful ones.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: A favor from Us; thus do We reward him who gives thanks.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: as a blessing from Us: this is how We reward the thankful.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: As a Grace from Us: thus do We reward those who give thanks.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: NiAAmatan min AAindina kathalika najzee man shakara

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: He had warned them of Our might, but they passed over the warnings.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And indeed he had warned them of Our seizure – in response they doubted the warnings.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: He had warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warnings.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And assuredly he had warned them of Our grasp, but: they doubted the warnings.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And he [Lout (Lot)] indeed had warned them of Our Grasp, but they did doubt the warnings!

36.author: Itani
36.text: He had warned them of Our onslaught, but they dismissed the warnings.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: Surely Lot warned his people that We shall seize them (with Our chastisement), but they doubted the warnings.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And he indeed had warned them of Our punishment, but they doubted the warnings!

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And he indeed had warned them of Our blow, but they did doubt the warnings.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: He had already warned them of Our punishment, but they disputed the warnings.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: He had warned them of Our assault but they disputed the warnings.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And he had already warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warning.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Lot warned them against Our torment, but they persistently disputed it.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And certainly he warned them of Our violent seizure, but they obstinately disputed the warning.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Lot warned them of Our punishment, but they disputed the warnings --

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: And (Lut) did warn them of Our Punishment, but they disputed about the Warning.

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: They lusted after his guests, so We put out their eyes (and said): "Taste My punishment and My commination."

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And they tried to persuade him regarding his guests – We therefore blinded their eyes, “So taste My punishment, and My warnings.”

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Even his guests they had solicited of him; so We obliterated their eyes, saying, 'Taste now My chastisement and My warnings!'

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And assuredly they solicited him for his guests: then We wiped out their eyes: taste then My torment and My warnings.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And they indeed sought to shame his guest (by asking to commit sodomy with them). So We blinded their eyes, "Then taste you My Torment and My Warnings."

37.author: Itani
37.text: They even lusted for his guest, so We obliterated their eyes. “So taste My punishment and My warnings.”

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Then they even solicited his guests from him, whereupon We blotted out their eyes, (telling them): “Now have a taste of My chastisement and My warnings.”

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And they indeed sought to shame his guests. So, We blinded their eyes (saying), "Then taste you My torment and My warnings."

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: They even asked of him his guests for an ill purpose. Then We blinded their eyes (and said): Taste now My punishment after My warnings!

37.author: Qarai
37.text: They even solicited of him his guests, whereat We blotted out their eyes, [saying,] ‘Taste My punishment and My warnings!’

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: They even solicited of him his guests, but We obliterated their eyes: 'Now, taste My punishment and My warnings'

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And they had demanded from him his guests, but We obliterated their eyes, [saying], "Taste My punishment and warning."

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: They demanded that he turn over his guests to them.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And certainly they endeavored to turn him from his guests, but We blinded their eyes; so taste My chastisement and My warning.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: they even wanted to seduce his guests -- but We blinded them, and said, "Taste My punishment now that you have scorned My warnings!" --

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: And they even sought to snatch away his guests from him, but We blinded their eyes. (They heard:) "Now taste ye My Wrath and My Warning."

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Walaqad rawadoohu AAan dayfihi fatamasna aAAyunahum fathooqoo AAathabee wanuthuri

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: And early in the morning the decreed punishment came upon them.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And indeed, the everlasting punishment overcame them early in the morning.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: In the morning early there came upon them a settled chastisement:

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And assuredly there met them early in the morning a torment settled.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning.

38.author: Itani
38.text: Early morning brought upon them enduring punishment.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: Indeed an abiding chastisement came upon them in the morning.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And in truth the punishment decreed befell them early in the morning.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: Early at dawn there visited them an abiding punishment:

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: And at daybreak a determined punishment came upon them.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And there came upon them by morning an abiding punishment.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: We struck their faces, blinded them and said, "Suffer Our torment of which you were warned".

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And certainly a lasting chastisement overtook them in the morning.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: and early in the morning the punishment decreed overtook them --

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: Early on the morrow an abiding Punishment seized them:

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: So now taste My punishment and My commination!

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: “So taste My punishment, and My warnings!”

39.author: Arberry
39.text: 'Taste now My chastisement and My warnings!'

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: Taste then My torment and My warnings.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: "Then taste you My Torment and My Warnings."

39.author: Itani
39.text: So taste My punishment and My warnings.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: So have a taste of My chastisement and My warnings.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: Then taste you My torment and My warnings.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: Now taste My punishment after My warnings!

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: 'Now, taste My punishment and My warnings'

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: So taste My punishment and warning.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: One early morning Our torment brought upon them utter destruction. We said, "Suffer Our torment of which you were warned".

39.author: Shakir
39.text: So taste My chastisement and My warning.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: "Taste My punishment now that you have scorned My warnings!"

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: "So taste ye My Wrath and My Warning."

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: Easy have We made the Qur'an to understand: So is there any one who will be warned?

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: And We have indeed made the Qur’an easy to memorise, so is there one who would remember?

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Now We have made the Koran easy for Remembrance. Is there any that will remember?

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: And assuredly We have made the Qur'an easy for admonition; is there then any one who would be admonished.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: And indeed, We have made the Quran easy to understand and remember, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

40.author: Itani
40.text: We made the Quran easy to memorize. Is there anyone who would memorize?

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Surely We have made this Qur'an easy as a reminder. Is there, then, any who will take heed?

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: And indeed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: And in truth We have made the Qur'an easy to remember; but is there any that remembereth?

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Certainly We have made the Quran simple for the sake of admonishment. So is there anyone who will be admonished?

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: We have made the Koran an easy Remembrance, is there any that will remember!

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: We have made the Quran easy to understand, but is there anyone who would pay attention?

40.author: Shakir
40.text: And certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance, but is there anyone who will mind?

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: We have made it easy indeed to learn lessons from the Quran. Is there anyone who would receive admonition?

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember: then is there any that will receive admonition?

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Walaqad yassarna alqurana lilththikri fahal min muddakirin

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: The warnings came to the people of Pharaoh.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And indeed the Noble Messengers came to the people of Firaun.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: The warnings came also to Pharaoh's folk.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And assuredly Unto the household of Fir'awn came the warning.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And indeed, warnings came to the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh) [through Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron)].

41.author: Itani
41.text: The warnings also came to the people of Pharaoh.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Warnings also came to the Pharaonites,

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And indeed, warnings came to the people of Fir`awn.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And warnings came in truth unto the house of Pharaoh

41.author: Qarai
41.text: Certainly the warnings did come to Pharaoh’s clan

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: The warnings also came to Pharaoh's people,

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And there certainly came to the people of Pharaoh warning.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: The Pharaoh and his people had also received Our warning,

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And certainly the warning came to Firon's people.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Surely warners came to the people of Pharaoh.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: To the People of Pharaoh, too, aforetime, came Warners (from Allah).

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: They rejected each one of Our signs. So We seized them with the grip of one mighty and powerful.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: They denied all Our signs, We therefore seized them – the seizure of the Most Honourable, the All Powerful.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: They cried lies to Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with the seizing of One mighty, omnipotent.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: They belied Our signs everyone thereof; whereof We laid hold of them with the grip of our Mighty, Powerful.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: (They) belied all Our Signs, so We seized them with a Seizure of the All-Mighty, All-Capable to carry out what he Will (Omnipotent).

42.author: Itani
42.text: They rejected Our signs, all of them, so We seized them—the seizure of an Almighty Omnipotent.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: but they gave the lie to Our Signs, to all of them. Thereupon We seized them with the seizing of the Most Mighty, the Most Powerful.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: (They) denied all Our signs, so We seized them with a punishment of the Almighty, All-Capable.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Who denied Our revelations, every one. Therefore We grasped them with the grasp of the Mighty, the Powerful.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: who denied all of Our signs. So We seized them with the seizing of One [who is] all-mighty, Omnipotent.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: but they belied all Our signs so We seized them with the seizing of the Mighty, Powerful.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: They denied Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with a seizure of one Exalted in Might and Perfect in Ability.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: but they rejected all Our miracles. So We seized them in the way that a Majestic and All-powerful One would.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: They rejected all Our communications, so We overtook them after the manner of a Mighty, Powerful One.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: They, too, rejected all Our signs. So We seized them with the seizure of One Mighty, Omnipotent.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: The (people) rejected all Our Signs; but We seized them with such Penalty (as comes) from One Exalted in Power, able to carry out His Will.

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Kaththaboo biayatina kulliha faakhathnahum akhtha AAazeezin muqtadirin

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: Are the unbelievers among you any better than they? Or is there immunity for you in the Scriptures?

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Are your disbelievers better than they were, or have you been given exemption in the Books?

43.author: Arberry
43.text: What, are your unbelievers better than those? Or have you an immunity in the Scrolls?

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Are your infidels better than these? Or is there an immunity for you in the Writs?

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: Are your disbelievers (O Quraish!) better than these [nations of Nuh (Noah), Lout (Lot), Salih (Saleh), and the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), etc., who were destroyed)? Or have you an immunity (against Our Torment) in the Divine Scriptures?

43.author: Itani
43.text: Are your unbelievers better than all those? Or do you have immunity in the scriptures?

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Are your unbelievers (of Makkah) any better than they? Or have you been granted any immunity in the Scriptures?

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: Are your disbelievers better than these Or have you immunity in the Divine Scriptures

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Are your disbelievers better than those, or have ye some immunity in the scriptures?

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Are your faithless better than those? Have you [been granted] [some sort of] immunity in the scriptures?

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: What then, are your unbelievers better than those? Or do you have an immunity (written) in the Scrolls?

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Are your disbelievers better than those [former ones], or have you immunity in the scripture?

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: Are you disbelievers mightier than those of the nations which We destroyed before or have you received amnesty through the ancient Scriptures?

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Are the unbelievers of yours better than these, or is there an exemption for you in the scriptures?

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Are your people who deny the truth better than those? Or have you been given immunity in the Scriptures?

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Are your Unbelievers, (O Quraish), better than they? Or have ye an immunity in the Sacred Books?

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Akuffarukum khayrun min olaikum am lakum baraatun fee alzzuburi

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: Or do they say: "We are a well-accoutred army?"

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: Or they say, “We shall all take revenge as a group.”

44.author: Arberry
