44.text: Or do they say, 'We are a congregation that shall be succoured?'

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Or is it that they say: we are a multitude prevailing!

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: Or do they say: "We are a great multitude, and we shall be victorious?"

44.author: Itani
44.text: Or do they say, “We are united, and we will be victorious”?

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Or do they say: “We are a strong legion, strong enough for victory?”

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: Or say they: "We are a great multitude, victorious"

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Or say they: We are a host victorious?

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Do they say, ‘We are a confederate league’?

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Or do they say: 'We are a gathering that will be victorious'

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Or do they say, "We are an assembly supporting [each other]"?

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Do they say, "We shall be victorious because we are united?"

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Or do they say: We are a host allied together to help each other?

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Or do they say, "We are a united group, and we are bound to prevail?"

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Or do they say: "We acting together can defend ourselves"?

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: The army will be routed and put to flight.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: The group will soon be routed, and will turn their backs to flee.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Certainly the host shall be routed, and turn their backs.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Anon will their multitude be vanquished, and they will turn the back.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: Their multitude will be put to flight, and they will show their backs.

45.author: Itani
45.text: The multitude will be defeated, and they will turn their backs.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Soon shall this legion be routed and shall turn their backs and flee.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Their multitude will be put to flight, and they will show their backs.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: The hosts will all be routed and will turn and flee.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: The league will be routed and turn its back [to flee].

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Most certainly their gatherings shall be routed, and they will turn their backs.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: [Their] assembly will be defeated, and they will turn their backs [in retreat].

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: (Let them know that) this united group will soon run away in defeat.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Soon shall the hosts be routed, and they shall turn (their) backs.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: The hosts shall soon be routed and they shall be put to flight.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Soon will their multitude be put to flight, and they will show their backs.

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Surely the Hour will be the moment of their promise, and that moment will be calamitous and distressing.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: In fact their promise is upon the Last Day – and the Last Day is very severe and very bitter!

46.author: Arberry
46.text: Nay, but the Hour is their tryst, and the Hour is very calamitous and bitter.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: Aye! the Hour is their appointed term, and the Hour shall be far more grievous and far more bitter.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time (for their full recompense), and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter.

46.author: Itani
46.text: The Hour is their appointed time—the Hour is more disastrous, and most bitter.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Nay; the Hour of Doom is their appointed time, and the Hour shall be more calamitous and bitter.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time, and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: Nay, but the Hour (of doom) is their appointed tryst, and the Hour will be more wretched and more bitter (than their earthly failure).

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Indeed, the Hour is their tryst; and the Hour will be most calamitous and bitter.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: Rather, the Hour is their encounter. And that Hour will be most calamitous and bitter.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: But the Hour is their appointment [for due punishment], and the Hour is more disastrous and more bitter.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: In fact, the Hour of Doom is the time for them to suffer. The suffering of this hour is the most calamitous and the most bitter (of all suffering).

46.author: Shakir
46.text: Nay, the hour is their promised time, and the hour shall be most grievous and bitter.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: Indeed, the Hour of Doom is their appointed time, and the Last Hour will be the most severe, and the most bitter.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: Nay, the Hour (of Judgment) is the time promised them (for their full recompense): And that Hour will be most grievous and most bitter.

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Bali alssaAAatu mawAAiduhum waalssaAAatu adha waamarru

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: Surely the sinners are misguided and insane.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: Indeed the criminals are astray and insane.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: Surely the sinners are in error and insanity!

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: Verily the culprits shall be in great error and madness.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: Verily, the Mujrimun (polytheists, disbelievers, sinners, criminals, etc.) are in error (in this world) and will burn (in the Hell-fire in the Hereafter).

47.author: Itani
47.text: The wicked are in confusion and madness.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: The evil-doers are victims of error and madness.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: Verily, the criminals are in error and will burn.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Lo! the guilty are in error and madness.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: Indeed the guilty are steeped in error and madness.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: Indeed the wrongdoers are in error and a raging fire.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: Indeed, the criminals are in error and madness.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: The sinful ones will face the destructive torment of hell

47.author: Shakir
47.text: Surely the guilty are in error and distress.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: The evil-doers are indeed sunk in error and folly --

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Truly those in sin are the ones straying in mind, and mad.

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: On that Day they will be dragged into the fire faces foremost, (and told:) "Taste the feel of Hell."

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: On the day when they are dragged upon their faces in the fire – “Taste the heat of hell.”

48.author: Arberry
48.text: The day when they are dragged on their faces into the Fire: 'Taste now the touch of Sakar!'

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: On the Day whereon they shall be dragged into the Fire upon their faces, it shall be said Unto them: taste the touch of the Scorching.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: The Day they will be dragged in the Fire on their faces (it will be said to them): "Taste you the touch of Hell!"

48.author: Itani
48.text: The Day when they are dragged upon their faces into the Fire: “Taste the touch of Saqar.”

48.author: Maududi
48.text: There shall come a Day when they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire and will be told: “Now taste the flame of Hell.”

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire (it will be said to them): "Taste you the touch of Hell!"

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: On the day when they are dragged into the Fire upon their faces (it is said unto them): Feel the touch of hell.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: The day when they are dragged on their faces into the Fire, [it will be said to them,] ‘Taste the touch of hell!’

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: On the Day when they are dragged on their faces into the Fire, (it will be said to them): 'Taste the touch of the Scorching'

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: The Day they are dragged into the Fire on their faces [it will be said], "Taste the touch of Saqar."

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: when they will be dragged on their faces into the fire and will be told, "Feel the touch of hell".

48.author: Shakir
48.text: On the day when they shall be dragged upon their faces into the fire; taste the touch of hell.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: on the Day when they are dragged into the fire on their faces, it will be said to them, "Now feel the touch of Hell!"

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: The Day they will be dragged through the Fire on their faces, (they will hear:) "Taste ye the touch of Hell!"

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Yawma yushaboona fee alnnari AAala wujoohihim thooqoo massa saqara

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: We have indeed created all things by measure;

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: We have indeed created all things by a proper measure.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Surely We have created everything in measure.

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Veriiy everything! We have created it by a measure.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Verily, We have created all things with Qadar (Divine Preordainments of all things before their creation, as written in the Book of Decrees Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz).

49.author: Itani
49.text: Everything We created is precisely measured.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: We have created everything in a determined measure.

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Verily, We have created all things with Qadar.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Lo! We have created every thing by measure.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Indeed We have created everything in a measure,

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: Indeed, We have created all things according to a measure.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Indeed, all things We created with predestination.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: We have created everything to fulfill a certain purpose.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Surely We have created everything according to a measure.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: We have created everything in due measure;

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Verily, all things have We created in proportion and measure.

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: And Our command is but one (word) swift as the winking of an eye.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: And Our command is only a fleeting one – like the batting of an eyelid.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: Our commandment is but one word, as the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And Our commandment shall be but one, as the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And Our Commandment is but one, as the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Itani
50.text: And Our command is but once, like the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: Our command consists of only one Word which is carried out in the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And Our commandment is but one as the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And Our commandment is but one (commandment), as the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: and Our command is but a single [word], like the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: And Our Order is but one word like the flashing of an eye.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And Our command is but one, like a glance of the eye.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: It takes only a single command from Us (to bring the Day of Judgment) and that can be achieved within the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And Our command is but one, as the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: We command but once: Our will is done in the twinkling of an eye;

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: And Our Command is but a single (Act),- like the twinkling of an eye.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Wama amruna illa wahidatun kalamhin bialbasari

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: We have destroyed the likes of you in the past: So is there any one who will be warned?

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And We have indeed destroyed your kind, so is there one who would ponder?

51.author: Arberry
51.text: We have destroyed the likes of you; is there any that will remember?

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And assuredly We have destroyed your likes; so is there any one who shall be admonished?

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And indeed, We have destroyed your likes, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

51.author: Itani
51.text: We have destroyed your likes. Is there anyone who would ponder?

51.author: Maududi
51.text: We did indeed destroy many like you. Is there, then, any who will heed?

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And indeed, We have destroyed your likes; then is there any that will remember

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And verily We have destroyed your fellows; but is there any that remembereth?

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Certainly We have destroyed your likes. So is there anyone who will be admonished?

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: And We destroyed those like you, is there any that will remember!

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And We have already destroyed your kinds, so is there any who will remember?

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: We destroyed many people like you (disbelievers) but is there anyone who would take heed (of Our warning)?

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And certainly We have already destroyed the likes of you, but is there anyone who will mind?

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: We have indeed destroyed many a people like you. Is there anyone who would receive admonition?

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: And (oft) in the past, have We destroyed gangs like unto you: then is there any that will receive admonition?

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Walaqad ahlakna ashyaAAakum fahal min muddakirin

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: All things they do are (recorded) in the books;

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: And all what they did is in the Books.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Every thing that they have done is in the Scrolls,

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: And everything they have wrought is in the Writs.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: And each and everything they have done is noted in (their) Records (of deeds).

52.author: Itani
52.text: Everything they have done is in the Books.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: All their deeds are recorded in the Scrolls;

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: And everything they have done is noted in Az-Zubur.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: And every thing they did is in the scriptures,

52.author: Qarai
52.text: Everything they have done is in the books,

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: All their deeds are in the Scrolls,

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: And everything they did is in written records.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Whatever they have done has been recorded in the Books (records of the deeds).

52.author: Shakir
52.text: And everything they have done is in the writings.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: All their deeds are recorded in their books:

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: All that they do is noted in (their) Books (of Deeds):

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: All things small or great have been written down.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And every small and great thing is recorded.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: and everything, great and small, is inscribed.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And everything, small and great, hath been written down.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And everything, small and big is written (in Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz already beforehand i.e. before it befalls, or is done by its doer) (See the Quran V. 57:22).

53.author: Itani
53.text: Everything, small or large, is written down.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: everything large or small, is duly inscribed.

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And everything, small and large, is written down.

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: And every small and great thing is recorded.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: and everything big and small is committed to writing.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: everything, be it great or small, is recorded.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And every small and great [thing] is inscribed.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: Every small or great deed is written down.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And everything small and great is written down.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: every action, small or great, is noted down.

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: Every matter, small and great, is on record.

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: Surely those who fear God and follow the straight path will be amidst gardens and light,

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: Indeed the pious are amidst Gardens and springs.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: Surely the godfearing shall dwell amid gardens and a river

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: Verily the God-fearing will be in Gardens and among rivers.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: Verily, The Muttaqun (pious), will be in the midst of Gardens and Rivers (Paradise).

54.author: Itani
54.text: The righteous will be amidst gardens and rivers.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: Surely those who shun disobedience will dwell amidst Gardens and running streams

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: Verily, those who have Taqwa, will be in the midst of Gardens and Rivers.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: Lo! the righteous will dwell among gardens and rivers,

54.author: Qarai
54.text: Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and streams,

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: Indeed, the cautious shall live amid gardens and a river,

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and rivers,

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: The pious ones will live in Paradise wherein streams flow,

54.author: Shakir
54.text: Surely those who guard (against evil) shall be in gardens and rivers,

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: The God-conscious will find themselves in gardens and rivers,

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: As to the Righteous, they will be in the midst of Gardens and Rivers,

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: At the still centre in the proximity of the King all-powerful.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: Seated in an assembly of the Truth, in the presence of Allah, the Omnipotent King.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: in a sure abode, in the presence of a King Omnipotent.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: In a good seat, near a Sovereign Omnipotent.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: In a seat of truth (i.e. Paradise), near the Omnipotent King (Allah, the All-Blessed, the Most High, the Owner of Majesty and Honour).

55.author: Itani
55.text: In an assembly of virtue, in the presence of an Omnipotent King.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: where they will be honourably seated in the presence of a King, Mighty in Power.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: In a seat of truth, near the Muqtadir King.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: Firmly established in the favour of a Mighty King.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: in the abode of truthfulness with an omnipotent King.

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: in a secure abode, in the presence of the Powerful King.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: In a seat of honor near a Sovereign, Perfect in Ability.

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: honorably seated in the presence of the All-dominant King.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: In the seat of honor with a most Powerful King.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: in the seat of truth with an all-powerful sovereign.

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: In an Assembly of Truth, in the Presence of a Sovereign Omnipotent.

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Fee maqAAadi sidqin AAinda maleekin muqtadirin
